_comment: Cabinet button + LED test map. Single source of truth, shared by the ESP32 firmware and the debug screen. Each button: `gpio` is the ESP32 input pin (wired to GND, read with an internal pull-up when `active_low`); `led` is `false` when the button has no addressable LED, or { "gpio": <pin> } once an addressable LED is wired. When LEDs exist the firmware drives them in a repeating cycle so each can be eyeballed. NOTE: GPIO 34-39 are input-only and have NO internal pull-up — a button on those pins needs an external pull-up resistor. If you rewire, edit this file AND the matching table in firmware/src/main.cpp.
